titleStmt.title: Letter from Ann French to Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, 5 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Ann French

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Ann French, secretary for the Irish Nurses' Association, to the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland (RCPI) informing them that the Nurses' Association has decided to take no action regarding the application of the Midwives' Act to Ireland. Enclosed with the letter is a copy of the resolution passed by the Association.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
THE IRISH NURSES' ASSOCIATION,
34 ST. STEPHEN'S GREEN,
Dublin, 5 June 1916 Dear Dr Kirkpatrick Your letters of May 30th to Miss Ramsden
was read at the Committee meeting on the 3rd inst.,
and it was decided to take no action as
regards the College of Nursing for the present. The enclosed Resolution was passed & I
was directed to send it to the Royal College
of Physicians. Yours truly Ann French
Sec. Maer C.
7.VII.16
THE IRISH NURSES' ASSOCIATION,
34 ST. STEPHEN'S GREEN,
Dublin, 3rd June 1916
Resolution re midwives act. Proposed by Miss Roberts seconded by Miss Reeves
& passed unanimously: " That the members of the
Irish Nurses Association have heard with great
satisfaction that the Royal College of Physicians,
Ireland have had a midwives Act for Ireland
under discussion, as they consider that this legislation
is urgently required." Maer C
7.VII.16
